title: Vela NegativeResult v0.1.0
description: A first-class kernel object for null results. The substrate-level shape that lets 'absence of effect' (an informative null with adequate power and a tight CI excluding the pre-registered MCID) and 'absence of evidence' (an underpowered or wide-CI readout) round-trip distinctly through downstream confidence math. Two variants by `kind`: `registered_trial` (statistical bounds) and `exploratory` (reagent + condition + observed-outcome tuple).

id.description: Content-addressed identifier. First 16 hex chars of SHA-256(kind.canonical() | deposited_by | created | normalize(conditions.text)), prefixed with 'vnr_'.

endpoint.description: Pre-specified primary endpoint (e.g., 'CDR-SB change at 18 months').

intervention.description: Intervention arm description (drug + dose, device + protocol, etc.).

comparator.description: Comparator arm description (placebo, active control, standard of care).

population.description: Population scope: indication, stage, age range, biomarker eligibility.

power.description: Statistical power for the primary endpoint, on [0, 1]. Below 0.8 is the 'underpowered null' that should not poison downstream confidence.

effect_size_ci.description: Confidence interval for the observed primary effect size, [lower, upper], in the trial's reported units.

effect_size_threshold.description: Pre-registered minimum effect size of interest (MCID), in the same units as effect_size_ci. When the CI excludes this threshold, the null is a positive epistemic claim about the absence of clinically meaningful effect.

registry_id.description: Trial registry id (e.g., 'NCT04532333'). Strongly preferred — the registry id is the load-bearing audit trail.

reagent.description: Reagent, compound, vector, or perturbation tried.

observation.description: Free-text observed outcome (e.g., 'no measurable expression', 'yields plateau at 6%').

attempts.description: Number of independent attempts whose outcome agreed.

target_findings.description: vf_* findings whose positive claim this null bears against. May be empty — exploratory dead ends don't always have a pre-existing claim to negate. Cross-frontier refs with @vfr_* suffix are accepted.

deposited_by.description: Stable actor id of the depositing lab / curator / agent.

conditions.description: Conditions under which the null was observed. Reuses the Conditions shape so model relevance, scope, and translation boundaries flow through.

provenance.description: Provenance of the trial readout / paper / preprint / lab notebook.

notes.description: Free-text reviewer note. Often the most important field — the 'why this null matters' or 'why we ran this in the first place' context.

review_state.description: Optional review verdict. Mirrors Flags.review_state on FindingBundle.

retracted.description: True once a `negative_result.retracted` event has been applied.
